category_title: Eighth-grade students with ASD displaying low academic engagement and task avoidance in math
minimal_context: The student is expected to complete practice problems in a peer-supported group during fourth-period math class.
dialogue:
TUTOR: It is time to complete the practice problems with your peer-supported group.

STUDENT: (Does not follow the directions within 10 seconds and begins doodling on the desk instead.)

TUTOR: Please start working on the math assignment.

STUDENT: No, I am not doing the work.

TUTOR: You need to do the practice problems with your group.

STUDENT: (Puts head down on the desk and pulls out a book to read instead of looking at the math assignment.)

options.A: Provide individual learning opportunities for the student to complete the math practice problems instead of using peer-supported groups.
options.B: Have the student turn in their checklist to the math teacher at the end of each class to receive an agreed-upon reinforcer.
options.C: Provide math assignments using large fonts with supporting pictures, reduced numbers of practice problems, word banks, and speech-generating devices.
options.D: Explicitly incorporate and promote self-compassion within the content of the student's math assignments and daily class activities.
